vocabulary.title: HHAeXchange FHIR API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain terms and definitions used across the HHAeXchange FHIR Onboarding, Internal, and Rate Limits APIs.

terms.definition: Numeric identifier for a homecare agency (also referred to as VendorID or CompanyID in certain HHA contexts). Used to map a FHIR client to one or more HHA database agencies.
Optional integer identifier for the HHA application version used in BaseUrl resolution. Defaults to 35 when omitted.
The resolved HTTPS base URL for a provider's HHA environment. Null for FhirPayer clients. Only populated when AppVersionId is provided.
Payer identifier stored in HHA's dbo.tblchhamaster table. Mapped as ClientMappings.AgencyId for FhirPayer clients.
UUID that uniquely identifies a FHIR client within the HHAeXchange platform. Generated on onboarding and used in all subsequent API calls.
Primary key of the FHIR.ClientMappings table row, uniquely identifying the combination of client, agency, and environment.
UUID credential returned once at client creation time. Must be stored securely by the caller; not persisted in the FHIR database.

Enum classifying the FHIR client: FhirProvider (agency/provider-facing), FhirAggregator (multi-provider aggregation), FhirThirdParty (third-party integrations), or FhirPayer (Medicaid payer/CHHA-facing).
HHA environment designator. One of app, app2, or cloud, corresponding to distinct HHA database mirror tiers. When omitted, the server auto-discovers the correct tier.
Electronic Visit Verification — a federally mandated system for recording homecare visits (caregiver, patient, date, time, location) to support Medicaid billing compliance.
Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources — HL7 standard used by HHAeXchange as the basis for its external API integration layer.
A FHIR client type that aggregates data across multiple providers. Uses the payer internal endpoint when a payer context is needed.
A FHIR client type representing a Medicaid payer (CHHA) that consumes visit and authorization data from HHAeXchange.
A FHIR client type representing a homecare provider (agency/vendor) that submits or queries EVV visit and caregiver data.
A FHIR client type for third-party systems that integrate with HHAeXchange without being a primary provider or payer.
HHA database table containing provider-specific reference code lookup data, keyed by CompanyID and ReferenceType (e.g., EmergencyContactRelationship).
Optional field in onboarding responses listing agency or payer IDs that were skipped (not found) or mapped with inactive status.
Numeric identifier for a payer (CHHA) stored as ClientMappings.AgencyId for FhirPayer clients. Maps to ChhaID in dbo.tblchhamaster.
Federal Employer Identification Number (FEIN / EIN) of a homecare provider agency, used to resolve agency-to-environment mapping.
Maximum number of API requests allowed per RateLimitWindow for a given client-agency-environment mapping. Defaults to 50.
Named rate limiting tier assigned to a client. Basic uses the default rate limits; Premium allows higher throughput.
Time granularity for rate limiting: Second or Minute. Defaults to Minute.
HHA internal identifier for an agency/vendor, equivalent to AgencyId in API contexts and CompanyID in database contexts.
AWS API Gateway x-api-key credential associated with a FHIR client. Returned at onboarding and used as a second credential factor alongside ClientId.
